data.title: Shell’s Arc Resources Deal Seen as Strategically Positive but EPS-Neutral, Kuplent Reiterates Hold and 3,500 GBp Price Target Amid Limited Upside
data.description:
In a report released today, Christopher Kuplent from Bank of America Securities maintained a Hold rating on Shell (UK), with a price target of p3,500.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Christopher Kuplent has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Shell’s planned $16.5bn acquisition of Arc Resources and its impact on financial metrics. He views the deal as essentially neutral for earnings per share and free cash flow per share through 2030, even after factoring in the company’s targeted cost synergies, and keeps his 3,500 GBp price objective intact while noting limited upside from current levels.

At the same time, he recognizes strategic positives, including a roughly 15% boost to group production, improved long-term resource life, and integration benefits for LNG Canada. However, his Neutral stance reflects investor uncertainties around Shell’s choice to fund about $10bn of the deal with equity, the implications for future use of its strong balance sheet, and how Arc’s sizable capital spending will fit within Shell’s unchanged $20–22bn capex framework.

In another report released today, UBS also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a p3,850.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: William Tng Raises AEM Price Target to S$10.15 and Reiterates Buy on Strong Earnings Upcycle and AI-Driven Growth
data.description:
Analyst William Tng from CGS International reiterated a Buy rating on AEM Holdings Ltd. and increased the price target to S$10.15 from S$4.86.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

William Tng has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, chiefly the onset of a strong earnings upcycle supported by a broader and higher-quality customer mix. He highlights that partnerships such as the one with ASE Technology, together with new AI/HPC and memory clients, should drive AEM toward the upper end of its revenue guidance and support meaningful margin expansion through FY28.

Tng also argues that AEM’s earnings growth profile, with an expected FY25–28 EPS compound annual growth rate of 83%, justifies a re-rating of the stock’s valuation multiple to be in line with sector peers. On this basis, he lifts his target price to S$10.15, seeing further upside if major customers accelerate equipment orders, while acknowledging risks from potential order delays, macroeconomic weakness, and ongoing litigation.

In another report released on April 24, DBS also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a S$4.60 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
